financial.note: KKP served as a lead underwriter and provides major credit facilities; interest rate hikes increase debt servicing on expansion CAPEX.
Provides trade finance and working capital; high sensitivity to prime lending rate fluctuations.
Holds ~1.18% equity; institutional backing provides liquidity support and price stability during market volatility.

supply_chain.note: Feed accounts for ~60-70% of livestock production costs; price spikes in feed grains directly compress GPM.
B2B and B2C channels allow for margin capture and better price pass-through compared to third-party wholesalers.
Major distribution partner for premium meat products; CRC's foot traffic drives BTG's high-margin premium segment sales.

competitors.note: Primary rival in integrated agro-industrial and food; competitive pricing in pork/chicken directly impacts BTG margins.
Direct competition in livestock and retail shop expansion; market share battles in the upcountry retail segment.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Input costs for animal feed; BTG typically hedges 3-6 months in advance, delaying the impact of spot price volatility.
Direct revenue driver; livestock prices are sensitive to supply cycles (El Niño impact on mortality) and domestic demand.
Net positive for BTG as exports (over 20% of revenue) benefit from THB weakness, offsetting the cost of imported soy meal.
Correlates with the performance of the premium food segment and 'value-added' processed products.
